title: Moral conviction and metacognitive ability shape multiple stages of information processing
description: This dataset comprises human electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from 80 participants engaged in a moral decision-making task. Participants evaluated their attitudes toward sociopolitical issues, then viewed protest photographs while receiving information about social support levels for each issue. The study investigates how moral conviction and metacognitive ability influence neural processing stages during social judgment and attitude formation.
methods_description: EEG recordings were collected from 80 participants who completed an attitude assessment task followed by a visual judgment paradigm. Participants viewed protest photographs preceded by pie charts indicating varying levels of social support (low, medium, high) for sociopolitical issues, and subsequently rated their support for the protestors.

funding_references.funder_name: Seed grant from Grossman Center for Quantitative Biology and Human Behavior (JD)
Grossman Center for Quantitative Biology and Human Behavior
